text:
There once was a wise old owl who lived along the edge of Whispering Woods. Every morning, she would gather berries and mushrooms, and every evening, she'd chat with the squirrels and rabbits as they danced around her nest. One crisp autumn morning, she heard a peculiar noise coming from the forest. Curiosity poked her wise old brow.

There was a young lion cub wandering in the woods, confused. He'd heard tales of this wise old owl. Determined to meet her, he huffed a little wind into his cheeks. The owl's eyes twinkled in the fading sunlight.

"Hello there!" she said with a soft, melodious squeak. "Just call me Olive."

The cub approached cautiously. "Hello... Olive?" he panted. His mane was slicked back like a mane of thunderclouds, but his eyes were kind.

Olive hooted warmly. "Hello, brave one! I didn't know you liked collecting stories."

The lion
